---
title: "Lecture 10. Feldman-Cousins, CLs, and related methods"
subtitle: "Statistical Data Analysis"
author: "Dmitry V. Naumov (JINR)"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: black
    width: 1024
    height: 768
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard:
      buttons: false
    logo: shared/figures/dvnlogo.png
    css: styles.css
    transition: fade
    center: true
---

## Draft Outline

- TODO: Neyman construction and ordering rules
- TODO: Feldman-Cousins intervals
- TODO: CLs method for limits

---

## Exercises

::: {.callout-warning .exbox appearance="simple"}
## Exercise

Show that the likelihood for a Poisson count $n$ with expectation $\mu$ is
$$
L(\mu)=\frac{\mu^n e^{-\mu}}{n!}.
$$
:::
